name: Chicken Broccoli Stir Fry
description: A quick and healthy stir fry with tender chicken and crisp broccoli in a savory garlic soy sauce. Ready in under 30 minutes and better than takeout.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, chicken broth, sesame oil, sugar, and 1 tablespoon cornstarch. Set aside.
Toss chicken pieces with remaining 1 tablespoon cornstarch until evenly coated.
Heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until shimmering.
Add chicken in a single layer and cook without stirring for 2 minutes, then stir and cook until golden and cooked through, about 3 more minutes. Remove to a plate.
Add remaining oil to the pan. Add broccoli and stir fry for 3 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Add garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return chicken to the pan and pour sauce over everything.
Toss to combine and cook for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens and coats everything.
Serve immediately over steamed rice.
notes: For extra flavor, marinate the chicken in 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tsp cornstarch for 15 minutes before cooking. Substitute any vegetable you like — bell peppers, snap peas, and carrots all work great.

name: Chicken Tetrazzini
description: A classic creamy pasta bake with tender chicken, mushrooms, and spaghetti smothered in a rich Parmesan cream sauce, topped with a golden breadcrumb crust.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Cook spaghetti according to package directions until al dente. Drain and set aside.

In a large oven-safe skillet or pot, melt 4 tablespoons butter over medium-high heat. Add onion and cook for 3 minutes. Add mushrooms and cook until golden, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute.
Pour in sherry and cook for 1 minute, scraping up any browned bits.
Add remaining 2 tablespoons butter and flour; stir and cook for 2 minutes.
Gradually whisk in chicken broth and heavy cream. Bring to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 3 minutes.
Stir in 3/4 cup Parmesan, salt, pepper, and thyme. Remove from heat.
Add cooked spaghetti, chicken, and peas. Toss to combine and transfer to the prepared baking dish.
Mix remaining Parmesan with panko breadcrumbs and sprinkle over the top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until bubbly and golden brown on top.
Let rest 5 minutes before serving.
notes: Great way to use up leftover rotisserie chicken. Can be assembled a day ahead and refrigerated before baking — add 10 extra minutes to bake time if cold from the fridge.

name: General Tso's Chicken
description: Crispy fried chicken pieces tossed in a bold, sweet-spicy sauce with a hint of ginger. A Chinese-American takeout classic made even better at home.
instructions: Whisk together sauce ingredients: soy sauce, hoisin, rice vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, chicken broth, and 1 tablespoon cornstarch. Set aside.
Toss chicken pieces with beaten eggs, then coat well in 1/2 cup cornstarch.
Heat 2 inches of vegetable oil in a wok or deep skillet to 350°F.
Fry chicken in batches for 4-5 minutes until golden and crispy. Drain on a wire rack.
Carefully pour off all but 2 tablespoons of oil from the pan.
Over high heat, add dried chilies and cook 30 seconds. Add garlic and ginger; cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the sauce and bring to a boil, stirring until thickened, about 2 minutes.
Add fried chicken and toss to coat evenly.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve immediately over steamed rice.
notes: For extra crunch, double-fry the chicken: fry once at 325°F, remove, then fry again at 375°F for 1-2 minutes. Adjust the number of dried chilies to control heat level.

name: Hibachi Chicken
description: Restaurant-style hibachi chicken with a savory butter-soy glaze, cooked at high heat for that signature smoky, slightly charred flavor. Served with fried rice and vegetables.
instructions: Pat chicken dry and season with salt and pepper.
In a small bowl, mix soy sauce, sesame oil, and lemon juice. Set aside.
Heat a large skillet or griddle over high heat until very hot. Add vegetable oil.
Add chicken in a single layer. Cook without moving for 2 minutes to get a good sear.
Add garlic and cook, stirring, for 3-4 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Add butter and pour soy sauce mixture over the chicken. Toss to coat and cook 1 more minute.
Remove chicken and cook vegetables in the same pan: add onions and mushrooms first for 3 minutes, then zucchini for 2 minutes.
Season vegetables with a splash of soy sauce and sesame oil.
Serve chicken over hibachi fried rice with vegetables alongside. Garnish with green onions.
notes: The key to hibachi flavor is cooking over very high heat. Use a cast iron skillet or flat-top griddle for best results. Serve with yum yum sauce on the side.

name: Kung Pao Chicken
description: A Sichuan classic featuring tender chicken, crunchy peanuts, and dried chilies in a perfectly balanced spicy, sweet, and tangy sauce.

ingredients.name: Chinese rice wine or dry sherry

instructions: Marinate chicken: combine 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon rice wine, and 1 teaspoon cornstarch. Toss with chicken and let sit 15 minutes.
Make the sauce: whisk together remaining soy sauce, rice wine, rice vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, chicken broth, and 1 teaspoon cornstarch. Set aside.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat. Add dried chilies (and Sichuan peppercorns if using) and stir fry 30 seconds until fragrant and slightly darkened.
Add marinated chicken and stir fry for 3-4 minutes until cooked through.
Push chicken to the sides, add garlic and ginger to the center, cook 30 seconds.
Pour in the sauce and toss everything together. Cook for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens.
Add green onions and peanuts, toss to combine.
Serve immediately over steamed rice.
notes: Adjust the number of dried chilies for your desired heat level. For authentic Sichuan flavor, do not skip the Sichuan peppercorns — they provide the signature numbing sensation.

name: Lemon Chicken
description: Bright and elegant pan-seared chicken breasts in a velvety lemon butter sauce with garlic and capers. A restaurant-quality dish ready in 30 minutes.
instructions: Season chicken with salt and pepper, then dredge lightly in flour, shaking off any excess.
Heat olive oil and 2 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook chicken for 4-5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through (165°F internal temp). Remove to a plate and tent with foil.
In the same pan over medium heat, add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add chicken broth and lemon juice, scraping up browned bits from the pan bottom.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes until sauce reduces slightly.
Remove from heat and whisk in remaining 2 tablespoons butter until sauce is glossy and emulsified.
Stir in capers and cream (if using).
Return chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over the top. Garnish with parsley and lemon slices.
Serve immediately with pasta, rice, or roasted vegetables.
notes: Pounding the chicken to an even thickness ensures it cooks evenly and quickly. Don't skip this step. A meat mallet or heavy skillet works great.

name: Moroccan Chicken
description: Warmly spiced chicken braised with olives, preserved lemons, and chickpeas in a fragrant saffron-scented broth. A deeply flavorful one-pot dinner.
instructions: Mix cumin, coriander, cinnamon, ginger, turmeric, and salt together. Rub generously all over the chicken thighs.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add chicken skin-side down and sear for 5 minutes until golden. Flip and sear 3 more minutes. Remove to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium. Add onion to the same pot and cook for 5 minutes, scraping up browned bits.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, and saffron with its soaking water. Stir to combine.
Return chicken to the pot, nestling into the sauce. Add chickpeas.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
Uncover, add olives and preserved lemon rind. Cook uncovered for 10 more minutes until sauce thickens slightly and chicken is very tender.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Garnish with fresh cilantro.
Serve over couscous or with crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
notes: If preserved lemons are unavailable, substitute 2 tablespoons fresh lemon juice and 1 teaspoon lemon zest, added at the end of cooking. This dish tastes even better the next day.

name: Chicken Shish Kabobs
description: Juicy marinated chicken skewers with colorful bell peppers and onions, grilled to perfection. Perfect for summer cookouts and easy weeknight dinners.
instructions: If using wooden skewers, soak in water for at least 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
Add chicken to the marinade and toss to coat. Marinate for at least 30 minutes or up to 4 hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat grill to medium-high heat (about 400°F). Oil the grill grates.
Thread chicken, bell peppers, and onion alternately onto skewers.
Grill kabobs for 12-15 minutes, turning every 3-4 minutes, until chicken is cooked through and slightly charred.
Chicken is done when it reaches 165°F internal temperature.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with fresh parsley. Serve with pita bread, tzatziki, and a Greek salad.
notes: Don't pack the skewers too tightly — leave a little space between pieces for even cooking. Yellow bell peppers and zucchini are great additions. These also cook beautifully on a grill pan indoors.

name: Artichoke Chicken
description: A simple yet sophisticated baked chicken dish with marinated artichoke hearts, garlic, and white wine in a savory pan sauce. Elegant enough for company, easy enough for weeknights.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Pat chicken dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken skin-side down for 5-6 minutes until skin is golden and crispy. Flip and sear 2 more minutes. Remove chicken.
Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic cloves and cook for 1 minute.
Add white wine and scrape up browned bits. Let it bubble for 1 minute.
Add chicken broth, Dijon mustard, and thyme sprigs. Stir to combine.
Return chicken to the pan skin-side up. Arrange artichoke hearts around and under the chicken.
Transfer to the oven and bake for 25-30 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F.
Remove from oven. Squeeze lemon juice over the top and stir butter into the pan sauce until melted.
Sprinkle with Parmesan and serve from the pan with crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
notes: Use good-quality marinated artichoke hearts — they add tremendous flavor. The reserved marinade can be drizzled over the final dish for extra flavor. Chicken breasts can be substituted; reduce bake time to 20-22 minutes.

name: Caramel Chicken
description: Vietnamese-inspired chicken thighs simmered in a rich, glossy caramel fish sauce glaze with ginger and garlic. Intensely savory, sweet, and utterly addictive.
instructions: Pat chicken thighs dry and season with pepper.

Add sugar to a wide, heavy pan or skillet in an even layer. Cook over medium heat without stirring until edges begin to melt. Gently swirl the pan and cook until sugar turns deep amber caramel, about 5-7 minutes.
Very carefully add water (it will bubble vigorously) and stir until caramel dissolves.
Stir in fish sauce and soy sauce. The caramel sauce is ready.
In a separate pan, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add shallots and cook until softened, 2 minutes. Add garlic and ginger, cook 1 minute.
Pour the caramel sauce into the pan with the aromatics. Bring to a simmer.
Add chicken thighs skin-side down. Cook uncovered over medium heat for 15 minutes.
Flip chicken and cook for another 15-20 minutes, basting frequently, until the sauce reduces to a thick glaze and chicken is cooked through.
Serve over steamed jasmine rice, garnished with green onions and sliced red chili.
notes: Watch the caramel carefully — it can go from perfect to burnt very quickly. If it smells acrid or looks very dark, start over. The deep amber color gives the signature bittersweet balance to the dish.

name: Slow Cooker Chicken Thighs
description: Fall-apart tender chicken thighs slow cooked in a savory herb and vegetable broth. Incredibly easy to prepare with minimal effort and maximum comfort.
instructions: Mix garlic powder, paprika, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper together. Rub all over the chicken thighs.
Optional but recommended: heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat and sear chicken skin-side down for 3-4 minutes until golden. This adds great flavor.
Place onion, carrots, celery, and garlic in the bottom of the slow cooker.
Whisk together chicken broth, tomato paste, and Worcestershire sauce. Pour over vegetables.
Place chicken thighs on top of the vegetables, skin-side up.
Cook on LOW for 6-7 hours or HIGH for 3-4 hours, until chicken is very tender and falls off the bone.
If desired, transfer chicken to a baking sheet and broil for 3-5 minutes to crisp up the skin.
Serve chicken with the vegetables and ladle some of the cooking juices over the top.
Pairs well with mashed potatoes, egg noodles, or crusty bread.
notes: The searing step is optional but highly recommended for flavor. Boneless thighs also work great; reduce cook time by about 1 hour. Leftovers are excellent shredded into soups, tacos, or sandwiches.

name: Sticky Honey Soy Chicken
description: Baked chicken thighs glazed in an irresistible sticky honey soy sauce with garlic and ginger. Sweet, savory, and caramelized to perfection with minimal prep.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking dish with foil and lightly grease.
Whisk together honey, soy sauce, garlic, ginger, rice vinegar, sesame oil, sriracha, and cornstarch in a bowl until smooth.
Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper and place skin-side up in the prepared baking dish.
Pour half the sauce over the chicken, reserving the other half.
Bake for 20 minutes, then brush with remaining sauce.
Return to oven and bake for another 15-20 minutes, brushing with pan drippings every 5 minutes, until skin is deeply caramelized and chicken reaches 165°F internally.
If desired, broil for the last 2-3 minutes for extra caramelization — watch carefully to avoid burning.
Spoon pan juices over the chicken and garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
Serve over steamed rice with a simple cucumber salad.
notes: The cornstarch helps the glaze thicken and stick to the chicken beautifully. Boneless skinless thighs work well too — reduce total bake time to about 25 minutes. The sauce is also great on chicken wings or drumsticks.
